author: @kathyreinhardt8998
text: I love the idea of adding iron filings to this mixture! I actually made a large batch of this stuff about 10 years ago to raise money for my son's Cub Scouts troop. We sold it in 4 ounce batches in sealed plastic bags. His troop made a lot of money! I have 2 grandsons who would LOVE this and, luckily, Borax powder is readily available here at any grocery store. I know the first thing they would do is find a way to drip it off their noses! Boys can be really disgusting.........also, you might want to let your viewers know that if this mixture is kept in an airtight container, it should last about 3 months or until the water starts to separate from the glue. Thanks Robert!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Kathy Reinhardt Hiya and thanks for that - the reason i keep the posts is because they so often contain such good info - thanks for the share

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: no it wasn't magnetic to start with - it was the stuff i made myself from a previous set of experiments - i did a video on it - i don't think it will permanently magnetise as it is held in a loose matrix and once the domains are aligned they would be held in alignment - i might try an expanded PU foam though - that might work.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: in small particle size magnetite is ferromagnetic as opposed to ferrimagnetic. This happens when the particle size is smaller than the magnetic domain.
